doc.field: The John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives (circa 1890-1933) consists of photographs and glass plate negatives documenting New York University's University Heights campus and Washington Square area. Materials in this collection were purchased from John Richards in 1938.

The John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives (circa 1890-1933) consists of photographs and glass plate negatives documenting New York University's University Heights campus and Washington Square area. Materials in this collection were purchased from John Richards in 1938.
Materials in this collection were transferred from the New York University Archives Photograph Collection (PHOTO 00001) in May 2024. The accession number associated with this collection is 2024.043.
Materials in this collection were purchased from John Richards in 1938.
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives

The John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives (circa 1890-1933) consists of photographs documenting New York University's University Heights campus. There are also photographs of the Washington Square area. Majority of the photographs depict buildings and the campus, but there are also some photographs of people.

This collection contains 13 glass plate negatives that mainly focus on Washington Square, including University buildings. There are also glass plate negatives taken by D.W. Hering of the Andrew H. Green plaque.
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives
John Richards Collection of NYU Photographs and Negatives
